reviews.review_msg: Somehow made ethics fun and easy to learn!
Somehow made ethics interesting and fun to learn!

4chan hoping for tenure. SFU phil must be desperate. 321 had so much potential to be interesting, but he wants to be one of the boys so bad he spends most of his lectures yapping and performing edgy temu comedy Also unironically refers to himself as a philosopher.......

Super nice and chill guy, but I am shocked by how disorganized and poorly run this course is. No notes, and doesn't write stuff on the board so he rambles for 2 hours straight (no breaks too). He's an easy marker, but compared to his previous courses, seems like he's phoning it in this time around. I hope this is just an off semester for him.

absolutely amazing in phil150, the content itself wasn't that interesting to me but i always looked forward to joining lectures just to hear him talk. he's hilarious and goes on tangents that are loosely related to the lecture but are casual enough to keep lectures interesting. he has a passion for what hes teaching, 100% would take him again

Funny, makes lectures interesting. He gave weekly assignments as worksheets but they were very minimal effort (~3-4 hours per assignment) and knew how to explain topics in a way that was relatable and applicable to modern life.
Not a good teacher

He radiates a youthful energy so lectures are fun, informative, and he constantly uses real life examples to make the concepts so much easier to understand. However, grading scale was wild: A+ = 97–100% , A  = 93–96%, A− = 90–92%, etc. All assessments are marked by TA. This course is only a GPA booster IF the content genuinely interests you.
peak prof, super understanding and kind, makes lectures very engaging and fun. Great sense of humor was the only class i looked forward to attending for a semester. Amazing prof for first years.

amazing professor, very interesting and hilarious lectures! only short weekly passages for hw and no final or midterm exam. Easy to get a high grade. Love this guy, 10000000% taking a course with him again

Absolute favorite professor! Nic is laidback, friendly, and very knowledgeable. Course was not heavy as it consisted of weekly questions, occasional assignments, and a take-home final (no papers), but still I felt very engaged and invested in everything that was being taught. I wish he taught more classes, would love to have him again!
amazing prof, this class became a gpa booster for me and i learned so much about asian philosophy, will definetely take this prof again if i get the chance to!
Amazing prof. Interesting content, informative lectures, and caring professor. You can definitely tell that he's super passionate about Buddhist philosophy. Would 100% take Nic's class again.

Course consisted of weekly responses and a final passage analysis assignment. Super chill prof, has a book which is used to as "textbook but is not required. Only problem with the course is the TA who marks difficultly. Otherwise I would recommend taking this course.

FAVOURITE PROF FR, no midterms just weekly assignments and his assignments aren't so hard as long as you put effort into it. Whoever the ta for this semester is i hope ur reading this and hope ur charger always work. You guys are an amazing team. TAKE 150 W THIS PROF YOU WILL LOVE IT

This has been one of the most interesting classes I have taken in a long time. Took it because I had a pretty heavy semester and just wanted something not super insane. Weekly reading assignments and an assignment to come but no exams, which has been nice.

Awesome prof!! His youthful vibe and style of teaching is amazing. Super understanding and the lecture material is very interesting. Submit the weekly assignments, do the readings, and you'll breeze through the class.

Nic's absolutely awesome, the lectures aren't mandatory but super useful. Didn't even need to use the textbook, just show up and listen and the weekly assignments should be a breeze. Not only easy to do well in, but actually interesting and engaging content and delivery. 10/10 would recommend.

Nic's lectures were engaging, informative, and straight to the point, all the while being laidback and humorous too. Genuinely looked forward to his classes every week. You can tell that he's passionate and knowledgeable in the material, which sparked my curiosity on the topics as well.

Professor Bommarito is an absolute gem of a prof. His teaching style is amazing--most of your grade comes from weekly reading responses from the texts covered in lec. Though some of the material can be tricky to grasp, he explains things so clearly with great analogies and examples, also throwing in jokes here and there! Absolutely rec taking him!

Super chill, funny, down to earth prof. Gives banger lectures and workload is relatively light: weekly responses to questions based off excerpts + a final project. No exams. Recommend to anyone who wants a strong intro to asian philosophy. *Note that the grade cutoffs are brutal e.g., got an A- with 95.7%. Avg will be high due to the class nature.

Asian philosophy (which turned out to be Buddhist philosophy) was so good with Nic. I would 100% recommend him, there are weekly/biweekly assignments but they take 5 minutes after the readings, which only take an hour max every week. I really enjoyed his lectures, uses many different examples for each concept, so really easy to grasp. 10/10

Nic provides an incredible model for all profs to consider when it comes to teaching online. His lectures were concise, the assignments were engaging and his book was incredible. While this class was somewhat easy, I truly believe most of that comes down to how he is able to motivate and interest you in the subject matter. An incredible author/prof
Nic was a great professor! Honest and caring, and he really wanted students to succeed in his class. Really like his approach to teaching and I highly reccomend taking a class with him.

I had never taken a class on Eastern philosophy and was scared I wouldn't understand anything. Nicolas explained everything in such a way that even someone who doesn't do philosophy would understand. He's a great professor who obviously loves what he does, and is extremely knowledgeable in his field. I would recommend taking any class with him.
